heading: Tweed Heads South vs Twelve Mile — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tweed Heads South, New South Wales and Twelve Mile, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Twelve Mile has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Tweed Heads South (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tweed Heads South or Twelve Mile safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tweed Heads South has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Twelve Mile scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tweed Heads South scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Twelve Mile scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tweed Heads South or Twelve Mile?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Twelve Mile has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
